initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['review']
Prompt family: review
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Write a draft answer and delegate one reviewer child to perform a rigorous technical audit. The reviewer must verify the accuracy of all claims, ensure all required components of the answer are addressed, and correct any logical or technical errors in the draft.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "review",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "draft_response": "plain-text draft answer",
  "message_for_reviewer": "plain-text review request for the child"
}

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: atlas-derived-A7-qd-hqec-mapping
Title: A7. QD-HQEC Mapping

Problem:
Is there a formal relationship between quantum Darwinism and holographic quantum error correction?

Grading target:
Mapping correctness on the three correspondences plus citation-gap discovery.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
final_prompt:

User prompt:
A review child has already returned critique or an improved draft. Use that returned work to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}

Child results already returned to you:
[
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_01",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: Yes, a formal relationship exists via three correspondences: 1) Redundancy: QD encodes system info in environmental fragments; HQEC encodes bulk info in boundary subregions. 2) Decoherence-Error: QD's environmental measurement process parallels HQEC's protection of logical information against local erasures. 3) Reconstruction: Reconstructing system states from environmental fragments (QD) maps to reconstructing bulk operators from boundary subregions (HQEC). Citation-Gap Discovery: While conceptual parallels are established, a unified dynamical derivation linking QD's decoherence rates to specific HQEC code properties remains an open research frontier.\nJUSTIFICATION: Correctly maps redundancy, decoherence, and reconstruction while identifying the dynamical gap."
  }
]
